headlines.title: Venezuelans dig for earthquake survivors as 72-hour rescue window nears end
headlines.snippet: At least 920 people are confirmed dead and more than 51,000 missing after two quakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday.

headlines.title: Thai police arrest Australian over killing of teenager found in suitcase
headlines.snippet: Thai police have arrested an Australian man at Bangkok's main airport as he was allegedly attempting to fly home. He is suspected of killing a teenager whose body was found stuffed inside a suitcase.

headlines.title: Germany braces for record-breaking temperatures as heatwave moves east
headlines.snippet: Germany is preparing for extremely high temperatures as a heatwave moves eastward. Several European countries, including the UK and Switzerland, have already experienced record June temperatures, with some fatalities reported in France.

headlines.title: Somali intelligence helps US arrest alleged leader of Minnesota fraud
headlines.snippet: Somali intelligence played a role in the US arrest of an alleged leader of a fraud operation based in Minnesota. US prosecutors sought the suspect in Somalia to bring him to justice for crimes committed in the United States.

headlines.title: Iran war day 120: Tehran condemns US strikes, says it violates MoU
headlines.snippet: Iran's foreign ministry condemned US strikes, stating they violate a Memorandum of Understanding. The US reported targeting Iranian missile and drone storage sites, while Iran claimed to have hit US military locations.

headlines.title: Israeli drone strikes southern Lebanon day after security deal
headlines.snippet: Israeli drone strikes southern Lebanon day after security deal An Israeli drone struck the Nabatiyeh area in southern Lebanon on Saturday, Lebanon’s state news agency reported.

headlines.title: Lebanon orders measures to prevent riots after deal with Israel
headlines.snippet: Lebanon orders measures to prevent riots after deal with Israel Lebanese Public Prosecutor Judge Ahmad Rami al-Hajj issued a judicial order for security forces to take measures to prevent riots, according to reports from Lebanese media.

headlines.title: Iran advisor says US violation of MoU will be met with swift and decisive response
headlines.snippet: Iran advisor says US violation of MoU will be met with swift and decisive response Mohsen Rezaei, an advisor to Iran’s supreme leader, said on Saturday that the US has violated the war-ending Memorandum of Understanding by creating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Iranian official says cargo clearance from UAE port resumes
headlines.snippet: Iranian official says cargo clearance from UAE port resumes An Iranian trade official told state media on Saturday that Tehran had resumed cargo clearance after US attacks on Iran following a drone attack on a cargo ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.snippet: Seoul reported that Chinese and Russian military aircraft entered its air defense identification zone. The report originated from Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Trump unveils new US passport – with picture of himself
headlines.snippet: Trump has unveiled a new US passport design that features a picture of himself. This news was reported by Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Iran says it struck US-linked targets as Bahrain reports attack
headlines.snippet: Iran says it struck in response to US airstrikes, with both sides accusing each other of violating an interim ceasefire, while Bahrain condemns a drone attack.

headlines.title: US strikes Iran sites after cargo ship attack, Tehran retaliates
headlines.snippet: US strikes on Iranian military sites, following an attack on a cargo ship, have raised concerns about security in the Strait of Hormuz and the stability of the ceasefire.

headlines.title: France’s terrorism apology law faces test in pro-Palestine case
headlines.snippet: A Paris court has halted the trial of Kazib, raising a constitutional question about civil party powers in a case concerning France's terrorism apology law. Campaigners view this as a victory for the Palestine movement.

headlines.title: Ms Rachel says world has failed Palestinian children at briefing
headlines.snippet: During a press briefing, Ms Rachel expressed emotional distress regarding the situation of Palestinian children. She apologized to a young girl and urged world leaders to take action.

headlines.title: Shia Muslims mark their holiest day in war-battered Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Shia Muslims observed their holiest day, Ashura, with marches through the ruins of Nabatieh. Mourners gathered to commemorate the event amidst destruction resulting from Israel's conflict in south Lebanon.

headlines.title: Egypt’s envoy to UN: Sudan bears consequences of ‘manufactured crisis’
headlines.snippet: Egypt’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Ehab Mostafa Awad Mostafa, said Sudan is bearing the consequences of a “manufactured crisis”, warning the international community against standing idly by in the face of the Sudanese people’s suffering.

headlines.snippet: Minister of Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Egyptian Expatriates Badr Abdelatty affirmed that President Abdel Fattah al-Sisi has given diplomatic work exceptional attention, reflected in the strong and active revival of summit diplomacy.

headlines.title: Al-Azhar offers condolences to Venezuela over devastating earthquake, urges global relief efforts
headlines.snippet: Al-Azhar expressed its heartfelt condolences and sympathy to Venezuela over the victims of the devastating earthquake that struck the country, leaving hundreds of people dead, while thousands of others have been injured and displaced.

headlines.title: Investment min: Faster customs clearance key to improving business climate, economic competitiveness
headlines.snippet: Minister of Investment and Foreign Trade Mohamed Farid said that reducing customs clearance times is a top priority in efforts to improve Egypt’s business environment and strengthen the competitiveness of the economy.

headlines.title: NATO chief declines to comment on Iran having ballistic missiles
headlines.snippet: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte declined to say if he agreed with President Donald Trump that Iran should have ballistic missiles.

headlines.snippet: Islamabad, IRNA – Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has expressed satisfaction with the recent visit of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to Pakistan, saying the outcomes of the trip will help strengthen bilateral relations between the two countries.

headlines.title: Iran, Pakistan move to boost bilateral trade as Taftan railway becomes customs station
headlines.snippet: Tehran, IRNA – Pakistan’s Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) has formally designated the Taftan border railway station as a land customs facility for import and export procedures, a move expected to facilitate rail freight, reduce transportation costs, and boost formal trade between Iran and Pakistan.

headlines.title: Ta’zieh performance on Ashura Day in Nushabad
headlines.snippet: The historical city of Nushabad in Kashan, central Iran, has hosted one of the country’s largest outdoor Ta’zieh performances on the 11th day of Muharram, the first month of the Islamic lunar year, attracting thousands of visitors from across Iran.

headlines.title: US assaults on Iranian coastal facilities violate war-end MoU: Tehran
headlines.snippet: Tehran, IRNA – Iran has condemned US air raids on several locations along the country’s southern coast, saying the attacks violated the recent memorandum of understanding (MoU) ending the war and reaffirming Tehran’s right to respond under international law.
